description: Generic linguistic markers that DISQUALIFY a next-step-shaped sentence (workshop / working session / pilot / proof-of-value / follow-up) from being treated as a forward-looking, accepted customer commitment. These detect a *linguistic shape* only — a past-experience recollection, skepticism about outcomes, or a direct negation of the activity — never a company, product, speaker, or a specific transcript's wording. The matching LOGIC lives in src/lib/qualification/nextStepPolarity.ts; this file supplies only the lexicon and is editable without code changes. A sentence that mentions a workshop/pilot/etc. but is really a customer objection ('we've seen service maps that look impressive in a workshop and become fiction') must not be promoted as an accepted next step, an agreed next step, a why-now driver, or a commitment.
